codeReview.summary: F019-FIX was never implemented. There is no worker session, no git commit, no code diff, and no transcript for this fix feature. The repository remains in the exact same state as the original F019 commit (90b50e2): Jest is still not installed or configured, the test script is still a no-op, and src/main/syncE2e.test.ts is unchanged—still heavily mocked and unexecutable. Consequently, none of the blocking issues from the original F019 review are addressed.

issues.description: F019-FIX has no implementation commit. The 'test' script is still 'echo "No tests configured" && exit 0', jest is absent from devDependencies, and no jest.config.js exists in the repository.

The test file is identical to the original F019 version (commit 90b50e2). It still mocks core dependencies (jest.mock('./syncDatabase') and jest.mock('./retryService')) rather than exercising real code paths, and remains unexecutable because Jest is not configured in the project.
VAL-FLOW-006 (Large Data Sync Performance) still only tests 100 records (Array.from({ length: 100 })), while the validation contract and feature expectedBehavior specify testing with 1000+ records.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The project still has no test runner configured. package.json lacks jest in devDependencies and the test script does nothing. The prior scrutiny validator synthesized fix features but no worker was dispatched to implement F019-FIX.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: package.json:14 — test script is 'echo "No tests configured" && exit 0'; no jest in devDependencies; Glob search for jest.config.* returned zero results.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission orchestration gap: features.json lists F019-FIX with status 'pending' and an empty workerSessionIds array, yet a review subagent was assigned. This indicates the fix was planned but never scheduled or executed before entering the review pipeline.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json shows F019-FIX status: 'pending', workerSessionIds: []; handoffs/ directory contains no F019-FIX handoff; git log --all --grep='F019' shows no F019-FIX commit.

summary: F019-FIX fails scrutiny because it was never implemented. No worker executed the fix, no commit was made, and the codebase is unchanged from the original failing F019 state. Jest is still missing, tests are still unexecutable and heavily mocked, and the 1000+ record performance requirement is still unmet. The original failures remain completely unaddressed.
